summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring a significant police employee recognition ceremony, including the swearing-in of the department's first-ever police chaplain, Pastor Aaron Ellermann. The board also addressed a resident's concerns about speeding on Shiloh Road, approved an OCJS body worn camera grant, awarded a $301,692 bid for roofing and building facade repairs funded by American Recovery Plan stimulus money, and approved a pre/post construction pavement analysis contract for the Villages of Belmont subdivision.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (March 9-22, 2022) passed
Motion to approve OCJS body worn camera grant in the amount of $10,713.76 passed
Motion to declare surplus/obsolete and sell refrigerator unit and soda machine on GovDeals passed
Motion to approve retroactive out-of-town training for Sergeant West for hostage negotiations training in Myrtle Beach, SC at $575 passed
Motion to reallocate $186,692 from ARP line item to correct ARP fund line item passed
Motion to award roofing and building repair bid to Garland DBS Inc. for total of $301,692 from ARP funds passed
Resolution R-020-2022 authorizing contract with Choice One Engineering for pavement analysis at Villages of Belmont subdivision for $2,400 passed
action_items: Chief Rose and Chad to look into potential solutions for speeding on Shiloh Road and report back to resident Dave Lockhart
Administrator to share Captain Hampton's awards ceremony livestream link on township Facebook page at 2pm the following day
key_amounts: $10,713.76 - OCJS body worn camera grant approved
$575 - Sergeant West hostage negotiations training cost
$186,692 - ARP fund reallocation for roofing project
$186,642 - Roof replacement at township administration/police station
$107,365 - Cleaning, repairing, and sealing of administration building brick and windows
$301,692 - Total bid awarded to Garland DBS Inc. from ARP funds
$2,400 - Choice One Engineering pavement analysis contract for Villages of Belmont
notable_topics: Resident complaint about dangerous speeding on Shiloh Road between Woodville and 727
Appointment and swearing-in of first police chaplain in 51-year department history - Pastor Aaron Ellermann
Police employee recognition: top drug interdiction officers for 2021
Detective Greg McAllister named Officer of the Year 2021
Recognition of Seven Hills Vet Clinic for free canine unit veterinary care
New body camera upgrades deployed to all officers
OCJS body worn camera grant for part-time position to manage body camera records
Major building repairs funded by American Recovery Plan stimulus money
Villages of Belmont subdivision - Drees Homes completing 90 patio homes with pre/post road inspection
Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association recognized for support
attendees: Officer Justin Porterfield (absent - on service call)
Harry (Planning/Zoning Director, at planning commission meeting)
notes: This was an unusually well-attended meeting due to the police recognition ceremonies. The meeting included a 15-minute recess for refreshments provided by the Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association. Harry (zoning director) was absent as the planning commission meeting overlapped. The administrator mentioned a recently deceased future firefighter in his mid-40s whose funeral was Thursday. Payment of bills dates were stated as 2020 but likely meant 2022. The oath of office incorrectly referenced 'Fairmont County' instead of Clermont County.
title: BOT 3.22.22.mp4
